senses.definition: the state or quality of being novel or fashionable; the world of novels or novels as a category
examples: She preferred the quiet charm of old libraries, far from the bustle of noveldom.
The author moved from popular noveldom to more serious literary work.

senses.definition: plural of noveldom; multiple people or groups associated with the world of novels
examples: The noveldoms crowd at the author signing were eager for selfies.
Her discussions with the noveldoms revealed a shared love of mystery fiction.

senses.definition: language that mimics the style of novels; prose in a novelistic manner
examples: The critic noted the novel's melodramatic novelese.
Her writing veered into novelese, with grand descriptions and ornate narration.

senses.definition: plural form of novelese; varieties or examples of novelistic prose
examples: The anthology includes several different noveleses from contemporary writers.
Readers debated which noveleses best captured urban life.

senses.definition: having the characteristics of a novel; strongly fictional or melodramatic in style
examples: The movie had a novelesque atmosphere with exaggerated emotions.
Her speech took on a novelesque tone, filled with dramatic twists.

senses.definition: a short novel or a short work of fiction; a novella
examples: She wrote a charming novelet over the weekend.
Publishers often look for promising novelets for magazines.

senses.definition: a writer who composes novelets; a writer of short novels
examples: The noveletist published several acclaimed miniatures.
As a novellist of novelets, he found quick publication in magazines.

senses.definition: a short novel or long short story; a short work of fiction
examples: The magazine published a collection of novelettes.
Her novelette won a prize for its clear writing and pace.

senses.definition: a person who writes novelettes; a writer of short novels
examples: The noveletter crafted poignant scenes within a compact form.
Many aspiring authors begin as noveletterists, writing brief stories.

senses.definition: plural of novelette; multiple short novels or long short stories
examples: The anthology contains several delightful novelettes.
Her favorite section features modern American novelettes.

senses.definition: resembling or characteristic of a novelette; short and melodramatic in style
examples: The film has a novelettish charm that appeals to some viewers.
Her dialogue carried a slightly novelettish quality.

senses.definition: a person who writes novelettes; a novelette author
examples: The novelettist found readers with compact, lyrical prose.
She admired the work of the local novelettist.

senses.definition: plural of novelettist; multiple authors of novelettes
examples: The conference gathered several novelettists from around the world.
Her peers included many talented novelettists.

senses.definition: having the style or feel of a novelette; light, short, and often melodramatic
examples: The dialogue had a faintly novelettish charm.
They enjoyed the movie's playful, novelettish atmosphere.

senses.definition: a writer who produces novelettes; a writer of short novels
examples: As a novice novelettist, he experimented with brisk plots.
The workshop helped several aspiring novelettists find their voice.

senses.definition: plural of novelettist; multiple writers of novelettes
examples: Several novelettists contributed to the anthology.
The meeting brought together many experienced novelettists.

senses.definition: like a novelette; having the feel of a short novel
examples: The film has a light, noveletty mood.
Her prose carried a quaint, noveletty cadence.

senses.definition: the process of turning a story or film into a novel; a novel adapted from another work
examples: The novelisation of the film added new scenes.
Fans bought the novelisation to relive the story.

senses.definition: plural of novelisation; multiple adaptations as novels
examples: Several movie franchises released novelisations and fan editions.
The publisher issued several novelisations of popular series.

senses.definition: to turn into a novel; to write a novelizing version of something
examples: The script was novelised into a tight, engaging book.
They decided to novelise the story for a wider audience.

senses.definition: past tense of novelise; turned into a novel
examples: The screenplay was novelised after the film's release.
She novelised several urban legends into short books.

senses.definition: a person who writes novels or turns works into novels
examples: She is a prolific noveliser of historical events.
The noveliser adapted the true story into a compelling book.

senses.definition: plural of noveliser; multiple writers who turn works into novels
examples: The conference gathered several well-known novelisers.
In interviews, novelisers discuss their process.

senses.definition: third person singular present of novelise; turns into a novel
examples: The studio novelises the screenplay into a book.
Publishers novelises various old legends each year.

senses.definition: having some features of a novel; somewhat novel-like
examples: The description has a novelish quality, but it's not a full story.
Her decision had a novelish charm, not quite realistic.

senses.definition: present participle of novelise; turning into a novel
examples: The team is novelising the events into a full-length book.
They are novelising several newspaper reports into a single narrative.

senses.definition: a movement or belief in novels; the practice or theory of novel writing
examples: The author's novelism emphasized character over plot.
Critics debated the new wave of novelism in contemporary literature.

senses.definition: plural of novelism; multiple movements or theories about novel writing
examples: Scholars discussed various novelisms across centuries.
The anthology surveys several novelisms in modern fiction.
